articles.title: DEI PUSHBACK AFFECTS LGBTQ+ MARKETING: HERE'S WHAT THE NUMBERS SAY
articles.summary: 62% of consumers support brands speaking on social issues, yet only 46% can define DEI, and 18% think it pushes an agenda. LGBTQ+ consumers, with $3.9 trillion in global buying power, have higher expectations, with 61% wanting brands to share commitments compared to 35% of the general population. Perceived LGBTQ+ representation in ads has fallen from 73% in 2023 to 65% in 2025. Scaling back DEI reduces trust for 41% of consumers and impacts purchasing for 39%, especially among younger and LGBTQ+ audiences.

articles.title: RESTAURANTS SEEK TO CASH IN ON CONSUMERS' PROTEIN FRENZY
articles.summary: The protein trend is changing US restaurant menus. Currently, 28.4% of menus highlight protein, up from 5.9% a decade ago, and this could exceed 40% by 2029. Driven by health-conscious consumers, including younger generations and those using weight-loss drugs like GLP-1, restaurants like Starbucks, Smoothie King, and Panda Express are adding or promoting protein-rich options to boost sales.

articles.title: TRUSTWORTHY SMILES
articles.summary: Using a genuine smile in a profile picture can increase approachability and reduce perceived risk, leading to more sales. Consistently using the same photo across platforms leverages Familiarity Bias, making people more likely to trust and choose you. Research shows people make judgments about trust in milliseconds based on facial cues like an upturned mouth and slightly raised eyebrows. One study found people invested up to 42% more in strangers with trustworthy features.

articles.title: FIRST YOU NEED AN SEO STRATEGY, NOT A GEO TOOL
articles.summary: AI will not kill SEO, but it will force it to evolve. Many marketers are wrongly chasing AI visibility tools instead of building strategies around user intent. SEO's purpose is to help people find answers, products, and services across any platform, whether it is a search engine or an AI agent. Ranking in AI responses is brand marketing and not a substitute for technical SEO and content that drives customers. The mentality needs to shift from gaming algorithms to optimizing for user needs and structured content that works across AI-driven search environments.

articles.title: ZERO-PARTY DATA IN ACTION: HOW TOP BRANDS TURN PRODUCT DISCOVERY INTO CONVERSIONS (WEBINAR)
articles.summary: This webinar will explore how brands like Free People, Centrum, and RMS Beauty are using zero-party data to drive higher AOV, more conversions, and retention. It will discuss the tactics used, including interactive quizzes, guided experiences, and personalized journeys. The webinar takes place on August 12 at 10 AM PT.

articles.title: STEAL THESE PROMPTS: 4 AI WORKFLOWS TO HELP YOU SHIP BETTER PRODUCTS
articles.summary: These 4 AI prompts break complex tasks into focused steps. They automate building feature taxonomies from a user perspective, turn product strategies into persuasive narratives, and frame initiatives within the overall strategy to connect work to business goals. They also offer critical evaluation from a leadership view to identify risks and alignment issues. While product-focused, these prompts are useful for marketers involved in messaging and strategy, helping clarify features and craft compelling stories.

articles.title: VUORI'S MARKETING STRATEGY: FINDING LULULEMON'S GAP
articles.summary: Vuori grew from a garage startup to a $5.5B brand in under 10 years by focusing on the overlooked men's premium activewear market. Rather than highlighting technical specs, it prioritized comfort and versatility, with fabrics like DreamKnit and BlissBlend driving word-of-mouth. A hybrid distribution model with partners like REI, Nordstrom, and Equinox built credibility and expanded reach while maintaining a premium image. Its success stems from redefining the category through cultural relevance, product innovation, and insight into how people want to live.

articles.title: ASSESSING BRANDING STRENGTH: COMPARING MARKETER JUDGEMENT AND CONSUMER DATA FOR BRAND IDENTITY ELEMENTS
articles.summary: Relying on intuition over consumer data can harm branding, as seen when Tropicana's packaging change caused a 20% sales drop. A study of 405 brand elements from 50 brands found marketers often overestimate brand fame and underestimate uniqueness compared to consumer views. Research showed marketers were only slightly better at judging logos and were more accurate in groups than alone.

articles.title: RANKED: THE COUNTRIES THAT SHOP ONLINE THE MOST
articles.summary: A ranking of the top 25 countries for online shopping, led by the US, China, and the UK.

articles.title: HOW TO SPEED UP CONTENT WITH AI
articles.summary: This 6-step AI-assisted framework combines in-depth research, voice transcription, and iterative AI editing to create polished content.
